user_stories.title: Parallel test execution with framework detection
user_stories.story: As an iSDLC user, I want test execution phases to automatically detect my test framework and run tests in parallel, so that my CI feedback loop is 2-5x faster.

acceptance_criteria.given: A project with a recognized test framework (Jest, Vitest, pytest, Go test, node:test, Cargo test, JUnit)
acceptance_criteria.when: An agent (05, 06, 10, or 16) executes the test suite
acceptance_criteria.then: The agent detects the framework and applies the correct parallel flag

acceptance_criteria.given: A project with a recognized test framework
acceptance_criteria.when: The agent constructs the test command
acceptance_criteria.then: The parallel flag matches the lookup table in the requirements spec

acceptance_criteria.given: A project with an unrecognized test framework
acceptance_criteria.when: The agent attempts to detect the framework
acceptance_criteria.then: The agent falls back to sequential execution with an informational message

user_stories.title: Agent prompt updates for parallel execution
user_stories.story: As the iSDLC framework maintainer, I want all test-executing agents to include parallel execution instructions, so that parallel testing is consistently applied across all workflow phases.

acceptance_criteria.when: The agent runs tests during build verification
acceptance_criteria.then: The prompt instructs parallel flag usage with the lookup table
The prompt instructs parallel flag usage with the lookup table

acceptance_criteria.when: Track A runs the test suite
acceptance_criteria.then: The prompt instructs parallel flag usage with the lookup table

acceptance_criteria.when: The agent runs tests during TDD iteration
acceptance_criteria.then: The prompt instructs parallel flag usage with the lookup table
The framework detection lookup table is present in the prompt

user_stories.title: CPU core-aware parallelism
user_stories.story: As an iSDLC user, I want the parallelism level to be automatically tuned to my machine's CPU cores, so that tests run as fast as possible without starving the system.

acceptance_criteria.given: A test-executing agent on any machine
acceptance_criteria.then: CPU core count is detected via os.cpus().length or equivalent

acceptance_criteria.given: A machine with N CPU cores
acceptance_criteria.then: Parallelism is set to max(1, N-1)
--test-concurrency=<N> is used with computed core count

acceptance_criteria.given: A framework with auto mode (pytest, Jest)
acceptance_criteria.then: Auto mode is preferred over manual core count

user_stories.title: Sequential fallback for flaky tests
user_stories.story: As an iSDLC user, I want failed parallel tests to be retried sequentially before being reported as failures, so that flaky parallelism issues do not block my workflow.

acceptance_criteria.given: A parallel test run with some test failures
acceptance_criteria.then: Only the failing tests are retried sequentially

acceptance_criteria.given: Tests that fail in parallel but pass sequentially
acceptance_criteria.then: A flakiness warning is logged

acceptance_criteria.when: Phase test results are written to state.json
acceptance_criteria.then: The flakiness warnings are included in the test_results

acceptance_criteria.given: A parallel test run with failures
acceptance_criteria.then: Only failing tests are retried, not the entire suite

user_stories.title: Parallel test creation for large codebases
user_stories.story: As the iSDLC framework maintainer, I want the test-design agent to spawn parallel sub-agents for large codebases, so that test generation for 10+ modules completes faster.

acceptance_criteria.then: The agent prompt instructs Task tool usage for parallel sub-agents
Each sub-agent generates tests for one module/domain independently
Cross-module test conflicts are resolved and test files are consolidated
The threshold (10+ modules) is documented in the prompt, not in a hook

user_stories.title: State tracking for parallel execution
user_stories.story: As the iSDLC framework maintainer, I want test execution results to capture parallel execution metadata, so that I can audit performance gains and flakiness trends.

acceptance_criteria.when: Test results are written to state.json
acceptance_criteria.then: A parallel_execution field is included with enabled, framework, flag, and workers

acceptance_criteria.given: A fallback from parallel to sequential occurred
acceptance_criteria.when: Test results are written to state.json
acceptance_criteria.then: parallel_execution includes fallback_triggered: true and flaky_tests list
A Parallel Execution section is included with mode, speedup, and flakiness summary
